frameworks.name: policy complexity / policy compression
frameworks.one_line: mutual information between states and actions as a capacity-limited resource

frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: RL with a penalty term on high-complexity policies

frameworks.name: rate-distortion theory
frameworks.one_line: information-theoretic framing of bounded behavioral capacity

frameworks.name: dopamine reward prediction error
frameworks.one_line: midbrain DA encodes a complexity-modulated learning signal

core_question: Does policy complexity shape midbrain dopamine reward responses during a perceptual decision task?
key_assumptions: behavior reflects optimal trade-off given capacity
dopamine encodes a modulable reinforcement learning signal
manipulations: reward outcomes in a perceptual decision task
key_findings: mice perform near-optimally given measured capacity limits
policy complexity suppresses midbrain dopamine responses to reward
neural and behavioral reward sensitivity positively correlated across sessions
interpretations: policy compression shapes basic RL mechanisms in the brain
complexity-penalized RL can be implemented via dopaminergic signal modulation
